title: Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf

seo.title: Japanese Milk Bread (Shokupan) Recipe | Soft & Fluffy Loaf
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Japanese Milk Bread (Shokupan) with our easy recipe. Achieve an incredibly soft, fluffy loaf using the tangzhong method. Perfect for sandwiches or toast!
longTailKeywords: how to make Japanese milk bread
best Japanese milk bread at home
seo.h1: The Best Japanese Milk Bread (Shokupan) Recipe
h2Headings: Step-by-Step Instructions for Japanese Milk Bread
Tips for a Perfectly Soft & Fluffy Loaf
seo.imageAlt: A golden-brown loaf of homemade Japanese Milk Bread (Shokupan) on a wire rack, showing its soft, fluffy texture.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the incredible softness of homemade Japanese Milk Bread (Shokupan) – perfect for toast, sandwiches, or simply enjoyed plain.

description: This Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf recipe yields an impossibly soft, fluffy, and tender bread perfect for any meal. Using the traditional tangzhong method, it ensures a superior texture and extended freshness that will make it your new favorite homemade bread. Enjoy it toasted, as a sandwich base, or simply on its own.

instructions.title: Prepare the Tangzhong
instructions.text: In a small saucepan, whisk together 1/2 cup water and 3 tbsp bread flour until smooth. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, for about 2-3 minutes, until the mixture thickens to a paste-like consistency (like wallpaper paste) and leaves trails when stirred. Transfer the tangzhong to a small bowl, cover directly with plastic wrap to prevent a skin from forming, and let it cool completely to room temperature (about 1-2 hours) or refrigerate for faster cooling.

instructions.title: Combine Dry Ingredients
instructions.text: In the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with a dough hook, combine the remaining 3 cups bread flour, granulated sugar, salt, and instant dry yeast. Whisk briefly to ensure all dry ingredients are evenly distributed.

instructions.title: Mix Wet Ingredients
instructions.text: In a separate medium bowl, whisk together the warm milk and the room temperature egg. Add the cooled tangzhong mixture to this wet mixture and whisk until well combined and smooth, ensuring there are no lumps of tangzhong.

instructions.title: Form and Knead the Dough
instructions.text: Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients in the stand mixer bowl. Mix on low speed with the dough hook until a shaggy dough forms. Cover the bowl with a clean kitchen towel and let the dough rest for 10 minutes. This resting period, called autolyse, helps hydrate the flour. After resting, knead the dough on medium-low speed for 8-10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. Add the softened unsalted butter and continue kneading for another 5-7 minutes until the butter is fully incorporated and the dough passes the windowpane test (a small piece can be stretched thin enough to see light through it without tearing).

instructions.title: First Rise (Bulk Fermentation)
instructions.text: Shape the dough into a smooth ball and place it in a lightly oiled large bowl, turning once to coat. Cover the bowl tightly with plastic wrap or a damp kitchen towel. Let the dough rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until it has visibly doubled in size. The exact time will depend on the temperature of your room.

instructions.title: Shape the Loaf and Second Rise
instructions.text: Gently punch down the risen dough to release the air. Transfer it to a lightly floured surface and divide it into 3 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a flat oval, then fold the top third down and the bottom third up, pressing to seal. Roll it up tightly into a log. Arrange the three logs seam-side down in a greased 9x5 inch loaf pan. Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap or a clean towel and let it rise again in a warm place for 45-60 minutes, or until the dough has almost reached the top of the pan.

instructions.title: Bake the Shokupan
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) about 15 minutes before the second rise is complete. In a small bowl, whisk together the egg yolk and 1 tbsp milk for the egg wash. Lightly brush the top of the risen dough with this egg wash. Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and an internal temperature of 200°F (93°C) is reached with an instant-read thermometer.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the loaf from the oven and immediately invert it onto a wire rack to cool completely. This prevents condensation from making the bottom soggy. Once cooled, slice and enjoy your incredibly soft Japanese Milk Bread. It's perfect for toast, sandwiches, or simply enjoyed plain.

tips: Ensure your tangzhong is completely cooled before adding it to the main dough mixture. This prevents premature yeast activation and ensures the best texture.
Achieving the 'windowpane test' is crucial for Shokupan. The dough should be elastic enough to stretch thin without tearing, indicating proper gluten development for a super soft crumb.
For optimal freshness, store your Shokupan in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days. For longer storage, slice and freeze, then toast directly from frozen.
article: Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf is a beloved Japanese Baking dish enjoyed across Japanese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
